creator: rene@FreeBSD.org
text:
I've got a build-tested patch, but the tool is now broken at runtime:

rene@e17:/usr/ports/sysutils/flyback % flyback
/usr/local/share/flyback
starting dbus-monitor...
dbus-monitor: unable to enable new-style monitoring: org.freedesktop.DBus.Error.AccessDenied: "Rejected send message, 1 matched rules; type="method_call", sender=":1.86" (uid=1001 pid=32894 comm="") interface="org.freedesktop.DBus.Monitoring" member="BecomeMonitor" error name="(unset)" requested_reply="0" destination="org.freedesktop.DBus" (bus)". Falling back to eavesdropping.
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/share/flyback/flyback.py", line 72, in <module>
    launch_select_backup_gui()
  File "/usr/local/share/flyback/flyback.py", line 43, in launch_select_backup_gui
    register_gui( select_backup_gui.GUI(register_gui, unregister_gui) )
  File "/usr/local/share/flyback/select_backup_gui.py", line 139, in __init__
    self.refresh_device_list()
  File "/usr/local/share/flyback/select_backup_gui.py", line 75, in refresh_device_list
    known_backups = backup.get_known_backups()
  File "/usr/local/share/flyback/backup.py", line 11, in get_known_backups
    for uuid in get_all_devices():
  File "/usr/local/share/flyback/backup.py", line 67, in get_all_devices
    return get_local_devices() + get_gvfs_devices()
  File "/usr/local/share/flyback/backup.py", line 63, in get_local_devices
    devices = [ os.path.basename(x) for x in os.listdir('/dev/disk/by-uuid/') ]
OSError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '/dev/disk/by-uuid/'
rene@e17:/usr/ports/sysutils/flyback % 


I'm not sure if we can ignore the dbus error, but the message about /dev/disk/by-uuid/ refers to a Linuxism which needs to be modified for FreeBSD.
